theme: sampler quilt where no two blocks repeat
title: Sampler Quilt: No Two Blocks Repeat
seed_problem: quilt-sampler-anticlone (B x B blocks of half-square-triangle b x b cells)
mutation: composite: block pattern-distinctness credited only up to a quota (dihedral-distinctness-quota), coupled to a seam-interface-continuity term counted across block borders, coupled to a hard global-color-balance feasibility gate that is decoupled from the pattern channel entirely -- so a solver must satisfy three simultaneously-active mechanisms with genuinely competing pulls on the same output.
strategies: trivial: one fixed all-\ pattern in every block (perfect seam continuity, 1 equivalence class) with a global continuous round-robin coloring -- reproduces the checker's own baseline exactly

naive greedy: an independent pseudo-random pattern per block (ignores borders -> seams match only ~50% of Smax by chance) with a coloring counter that RESTARTS every block -- on tests where c does not divide b*b this restart bias compounds over B*B blocks and blows the color-balance tolerance, scoring 0

insight (strong): fix every block's border ring to a single constant so all seams match automatically (Smax), then enumerate the free interior cells in canonical-form order (dihedral quotient via the 8 symmetries of the square) to certify Q pairwise-distinct block patterns cheaply, using a global continuous-counter round-robin for colors so the balance gate is always satisfied regardless of B
